home.subtitle: CI/CD automation bot
home.heroDescription: Automate your GitHub workflows with intelligent CI checks, detailed PR reports, and secure webhook handling. Built for teams that value reliability.
home.featuresSubtitle: Everything you need to streamline your development workflow
home.featureCicdDesc: Automated build verification and quality checks on every push. Catch issues early with comprehensive status reporting.
home.featurePrDesc: Detailed pull request summaries with build status, test results, and actionable feedback posted directly to GitHub.
home.featureWebhookDesc: HMAC signature verification ensures every webhook payload is authentic and tamper-proof. Security by default.
home.howItWorksSubtitle: Four simple steps to automated CI/CD
home.step1Desc: Configure a GitHub App and connect Manticore Bot to your repository
home.step2Desc: CI pipeline triggers automatically on every push or pull request
home.step3Desc: Runs dependency install, lint, type check, and build verification in sequence
home.step4Desc: Structured build reports are posted directly to your PR as comments
home.techStackSubtitle: Built on proven technologies for stability and reliability
home.ctaSubtitle: Add intelligent CI/CD automation to your GitHub repositories
home.footerDesc: Open-source CI/CD automation bot, built for teams that value reliability
home.installAppDesc: Install Manticore Bot on your GitHub account or organization to authorize repository access
home.noInstallationsDesc: Install the GitHub App to manage CI/CD automation for your repositories
home.appNotConfiguredDesc: Please ask the admin to configure GITHUB_APP_ID and GITHUB_APP_SLUG environment variables

envPage.title: Environment Variables
envPage.subtitle: View configuration status and usage of all environment variables

envError.title: Environment Variables Missing
envError.description: Application cannot run normally because necessary environment variables are missing.
envError.step1: Create .env.local file in project root directory
envError.step2: Add the missing environment variable configuration
envError.step4: Refer to .env.example file for complete configuration reference

envVarDescriptions.githubClientId: GitHub OAuth App Client ID, used for user login authentication
envVarDescriptions.githubClientSecret: GitHub OAuth App Client Secret, used to exchange access tokens
envVarDescriptions.jwtSecret: JWT signing secret, used to generate and verify user session tokens
envVarDescriptions.encryptionKey: AES-256-GCM data encryption key, used for encrypting sensitive information
envVarDescriptions.githubAppId: GitHub App numeric ID, used for generating App JWT authentication
envVarDescriptions.githubAppSlug: GitHub App URL identifier, used for generating installation links
envVarDescriptions.githubPrivateKeyPath: Path to GitHub App private key file (.pem)
envVarDescriptions.webhookSecret: Webhook signature verification secret, used to verify GitHub Webhook requests
envVarDescriptions.repoOwner: Target repository owner (GitHub username or organization name)
envVarDescriptions.collaborators: Users allowed to manually trigger CI retry (comma-separated)
envVarDescriptions.workspaceDir: Git workspace directory path, used for code checkout during CI checks
envVarDescriptions.appUrl: Public access URL of the application

envUsage.githubClientId: When user clicks 'Login with GitHub', redirects to GitHub authorization page. After authorization, obtains access token to complete login
envUsage.githubClientSecret: Exchanges authorization code for access_token in /api/auth/callback, fetches user info and issues JWT
envUsage.jwtSecret: Issues auth_token cookie (HttpOnly, 7-day expiry). All authenticated API requests verify identity through this token
envUsage.encryptionKey: Prisma encryption middleware automatically encrypts/decrypts sensitive database fields (Webhook Secret, private keys, etc.)
envUsage.databaseUrl: Prisma Client connects to database, stores admin accounts, GitHub App installations, CI build history, etc.
envUsage.githubAppId: Works with GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH to generate RS256-signed JWT, obtains Installation Access Token for GitHub API calls
envUsage.githubAppSlug: Dashboard 'Install GitHub App' button uses this variable to generate installation link: https://github.com/apps/{SLUG}/installations/new
envUsage.githubPrivateKeyPath: Used to generate GitHub App JWT (RS256 signature), obtains Installation Access Token. Private key downloaded when creating GitHub App
envUsage.webhookSecret: Used in /api/webhook/github with HMAC-SHA256 to verify x-hub-signature-256 header, prevents forged Webhook requests
envUsage.repoOwner: Used during CI pipeline execution to fetch PR info from GitHub API and post build report comments on PRs
envUsage.repoName: Works with REPO_OWNER to locate the target repository for CI/CD operations
envUsage.collaborators: When user types /rebuild or /retry in PR comment, verifies commenter is in this list to prevent unauthorized CI triggers
envUsage.workspaceDir: Target directory for git fetch/checkout/merge operations during CI pipeline execution. Defaults to current directory
envUsage.appUrl: Used for generating OAuth callback URLs and other scenarios requiring the full application address

envCategory.oauthDesc: GitHub OAuth App configuration required for user login authentication
envCategory.securityDesc: Security keys required for JWT signing and data encryption
envCategory.githubAppDesc: GitHub App installation and API call configuration

envVarHints.githubClientId:
1. Go to https://github.com/settings/developers
2. Click "New OAuth App"
3. Fill in: Application name = Manticore Bot, Homepage URL = your domain, Callback URL = your-domain/api/auth/callback
4. Click "Register application"
5. Copy the Client ID shown on the page
envVarHints.githubClientSecret:
1. On the OAuth App page (same as above)
2. Click "Generate a new client secret"
3. Copy the secret value immediately (shown only once!)
4. If lost, delete and generate a new one
envVarHints.jwtSecret:
Run in terminal:
openssl rand -hex 32

Then copy the 64-character hex string to .env.local
envVarHints.encryptionKey:
Run in terminal:
openssl rand -hex 32

Then copy the 64-character hex string to .env.local
envVarHints.databaseUrl:
Option A - Docker (recommended):
docker run -d --name postgres -e POSTGRES_USER=user -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password -e POSTGRES_DB=manticore -p 5432:5432 postgres:16-alpine

Then set: postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/manticore

Option B - Local PostgreSQL:
1. Install: sudo apt install postgresql
2. Create DB: psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE manticore;"
3. Set connection string accordingly
envVarHints.githubAppId:
Steps:
1. Go to https://github.com/settings/apps
2. Click on your App name
3. The 'GitHub App ID' in the 'About' section at the top is the value you need
4. Or check the URL format: https://github.com/settings/apps/{slug}
envVarHints.githubAppSlug:
Steps:
1. Go to https://github.com/settings/apps
2. Click on your App name
3. The name in the page URL is the slug
4. Or visible below 'GitHub App name' in App settings
5. Installation link format: https://github.com/apps/{slug}/installations/new
envVarHints.githubPrivateKeyPath:
Steps:
1. In GitHub App settings, scroll to 'Private keys' section at the bottom
2. Click 'Generate a private key'
3. Download the .pem file
4. Place the file on your server, e.g.: /app/private-key.pem
5. Enter the absolute path or path relative to project root
envVarHints.webhookSecret:
Steps:
1. In GitHub App settings, go to 'Webhooks' section
2. If set during creation, use that secret
3. To generate a new one, run in terminal: openssl rand -base64 32
4. Enter the same secret in both .env and GitHub App webhook settings
5. Both must match exactly
envVarHints.repoOwner:
Enter your GitHub username or organization name.

Examples:
- Personal: Jyf0214
- Organization: my-org-name

Find it in the repository URL: https://github.com/{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}
envVarHints.collaborators:
Comma-separated list of users allowed to trigger CI retry via PR comments.

Example: user1,user2,bot-account

These users can type /rebuild or /retry in PR comments to manually trigger CI checks.

Leave empty to disable manual triggers.
envVarHints.workspaceDir:
Git workspace directory for CI checks.

Default: current directory (project root)

In Docker deployments, mount a dedicated directory via docker-compose.yml volumes, e.g.: ./workspace
envVarHints.appUrl:
Public access URL of the application, used for generating OAuth callback URLs.

Examples:
- Local dev: http://localhost:3001
- Production: https://manticore-bot.example.com

Note: do not end with /

api.unauthorized: Only administrators can access this application
api.invalidPath: Private key file path is invalid
api.invalidFormat: Private key file format is invalid
